data.title: Kroger launches $7.5B stock buyback after merger with Albertsons gets terminated
data.description:
Kroger (NYSE:KR) on Wednesday announced a $7.5B stock buyback in the wake of its terminated merger with Albertsons (NYSE:ACI).

The new buyback plan replaces the company's existing $1B authorization, which was approved in September 2022, and includes an "accelerated share repurchase" agreement to buy back about $5B in stock.

"Now that Kroger has terminated the merger agreement, the company is ready to deploy its capacity. With its strengthened balance sheet, Kroger will resume share repurchases after a more than two-year pause," the company said in a statement.

Kroger said in connection with the termination of the merger agreement, it will begin redeeming the $4.7B of its senior notes issued on August 27, 2024, which includes a special mandatory redemption provision in accordance with the terms of the notes.

The company is also terminating its previously announced offers to exchange any and all outstanding notes issued by Albertsons of up to $7.4B.

KR shares are up nearly 2% in the afterhours of trading on the NYSE.

data.title: Microsoft faces $800M charge for investment in GM's Cruise
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) expects to incur an $800M impairment charge due to a 2021 investment in Cruise, a robotaxi startup which is majority owned by General Motors (NYSE:GM).
  • On Tuesday, General Motors announced it no longer plans to fund Cruise, and intends to pursue the acquisition of minority investor shares.
  • "As a result, we expect to record an impairment charge of approximately $800 million in the second quarter of fiscal year 2025," according to a Form 8-K. "This charge will be recorded in other income and expense and was not included in our second quarter guidance provided on October 30, 2024. It is estimated to have a negative impact of approximately $0.09 to second quarter diluted earnings per share."
  • General Motors said it is shifting funds from Cruise to focus on autonomous vehicle development for personally owned vehicles.

data.title: Marsh & McLennan raised to Outperform as RBC gets bullish heading into 2025
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets upgraded Marsh & McLennan (NYSE:MMC) stock to Outperform from Sector Perform on Wednesday on the view that there is "good visibility into solid top and bottom-line growth as we head into 2025."

In addition to a pick-up in revenue growth in its Risk & Insurance Services unit and improved margins, the insurance company is less sensitive to P&C pricing and positioned for any softening, analyst Scott Heleniak wrote in a note.

On valuation, Heleniak noted that (NYSE:MMC) is changing hands at 22x his 2025 EPS forecast vs. 24x for peers.

"We think that MMC should trade closer to peers based on consistently delivering strong top and bottom-line growth, successful M&A, and an active return program," the note said.

RBC's Outperform rate diverges from the SA Quant system rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Hold.

data.title: Community Health Systems to sell North Carolina hospital to Duke Health
data.description:
• Community Health Systems (NYSE:CYH) on Wednesday said a subsidiary of the company has signed an agreement to sell 123-bed Lake Norman Regional Medical Center in North Carolina and related businesses, to Duke Health for about $280 million.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Diebold Nixdorf prices $950M notes offering at 100% of principal amount
data.description:
• Diebold Nixdorf (NYSE:DBD) priced its $950M senior secured notes offering at 100.000% of their principal amount.
  • The 7.750% senior secured notes are due 2030.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 18.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Robinhood says trading volume rose 16% in November
data.description:
Equity notional trading volumes were $147.1B in November, up 16% M/M and 178% Y/Y, while crypto notional trading volumes of $35.2B surged sixfold from October and eightfold from November 2023, the stock and crypto trading app company said on Wednesday.

Options contracts traded were 155.5M, down 2% M/M and up 63% Y/Y.

Funded customers at the end of November were 24.8M, up 420K from October 2024 and 1.5M from a year ago.

Assets under custody at the end of the month rose 22% M/M to $195B, representing a 106% increase Y/Y. Net deposits of $5.6B translated to a 42% annualized growth rate relative to October 2024 AUC. Over the last 12 months, net deposits were $47.4B, representing an annual growth rate of 50%.

Margin balances at the end of the month were $6.8B, up 10% M/M and 100% Y/Y.

Total cash sweep balances were $26.5B at the end of November, up 4% M/M and 74% Y/Y.

Total securities lending revenue increased 21% M/M to $23M, rising 109% on a Y/Y basis.

Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) stock rose 0.3% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Coca-Cola appoints Henrique Braun as COO
data.description:
• The Coca-Cola Company (NYSE:KO) on Wednesday announced that Henrique Braun has been appointed as executive vice president and chief operating officer (COO), effective Jan. 1, 2025.
  • Braun will be responsible for all the company’s operating units worldwide, the company said.
  • Braun currently serves as EVP and President, International Development, overseeing the company’s operating units for Latin America; Japan & South Korea; ASEAN & South Pacific; Greater China and Mongolia; Africa; India & Southwest Asia; and Eurasia and Middle East.
  • KO -0.04% after hours to $62.61.
  • Source: Press Release
